---
title: "The LTS Document: Lab, Template and Solutions All in One"
author: William Hopper
image: featured.png
image-alt: "Screenshot from Colin's presentation with parsermd package logo overlaid."
---

## Abstract

Assigning low-stakes practice problems or "lab" activities during class is one strategy for incorporating active learning into a traditional lecture-oriented course. 
In a course that incorporates computation, instructors may find it useful to provide a document with detailed information and instructions (a "lab" document), a document with initial scaffolding for students to work within (a "template" document), and a document with example results (a "solutions" document). 
However, keeping content synchronized across these three documents, as well as the learning management system used to distribute these documents, introduces administrative overhead and mistakes. 
This talk demonstrates how to use an LTS document (a custom document format for the Quarto publishing system) to create each of these student-facing files simultaneously from one main instructor-facing file, and automatically deploy them to Moodle using an R package. 

## Slides

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="" height="420" width="747" style="border: 1px solid #2e3846;"></iframe>
```

[Source code for slides]()

[Full screen slides]()

## Speaker

![](https://www.smith.edu/sites/default/files/styles/content_faculty_profile_full_large/public/2025-09/will-hopper-25.jpg.webp?itok=xsaslWSL){style="float: right; padding-left: 10px;" fig-alt="Photo of Colin Rundel" width="200"}

Will Hopper is...
